title: लक्ष्मी नरसिंह स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मी नरसिंह स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
लक्ष्मीकान्तं नृसिंहं च सर्वलोकैकनायकम्।
वामाङ्के लक्ष्मीसहितं ध्यायेन्नित्यं हरिं परम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
श्रीमत्पयोनिधिनिकेतन चक्रपाणे
भोगीन्द्रभोगमणिराजितपुण्यमूर्ते।
योगीश शाश्वत शरण्य भवाब्धिपोत
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ १॥

ब्रह्मेन्द्ररुद्रमरुदर्ककिरीटकोटि-
संघट्टिताङ्घ्रिकमलामलकान्तिकान्त।
लक्ष्मीलसत्कुचसरोरुहराजहंस
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ २॥

संसारदावदहनातुरभीकरोरु-
ज्वालावलीभिरतिदग्धतनूरुहस्य।
त्वत्पादपद्मसरसीरुहमागतस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ३॥

संसारजालपतितस्य जगन्निवास
सर्वेन्द्रियार्थबडिशाग्रझषोपमस्य।
प्रोत्कम्पितप्रचुरतालुकमस्तकस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ४॥

संसारकूपमतिघोरमगाधमूलं
सम्प्राप्य दुःखशतसर्पसमाकुलस्य।
दीनस्य देव कृपया पदमागतस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ५॥

संसारभीकरकरीन्द्रकराभिघात-
निष्पीड्यमानवपुषः सकलार्तिनाश।
प्राणप्रयाणभवभीतिसमाकुलस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ६॥

संसारसर्पविषदिग्धमहोग्रतीव्र-
दंष्ट्राग्रकोटिपरिदष्टविनष्टमूर्तेः।
nागारिवाहन सुधाब्धिनिवास शौरे
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ७॥

संसारवृक्षमघबीजमनन्तकर्म-
शाखायुतं करणपत्रमनंगपुष्पम्।
आरुह्य दुःखफलितं पततो दयालो
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
लक्ष्मीनृसिंहचरणाब्जमधुव्रतेन
स्तोत्रं कृतं शुभकरं भुवि शङ्करेण।
ये तत्पठन्ति मनुजा हरिभक्तियुक्ताः
ते यान्ति तत्पदसरोजमखण्डरूपम्॥

॥ इति श्री लक्ष्मी नरसिंह करावलम्ब स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Lakshmi-kantam Nrisimham Cha Sarvaloka-eka-nayakam.
Vamange Lakshmi-sahitam Dhyayen-nityam Harim Param.

|| Stotram ||
Shrimat-payonidhi-niketana Chakrapane
Bhogindra-bhoga-mani-rajita-punya-murte.
Yogisha Shashvata Sharanya Bhavabdhi-pota
Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam. || 1 ||

Brahma-indra-rudra-marud-arka-kirita-koti-
Sanghatita-anghri-kamala-amala-kanti-kanta.
Lakshmi-lasat-kucha-saroruha-raja-hamsa
Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam. || 2 ||

Samsara-dava-dahana-atura-bhikara-uru-
Jwala-avalibhir-ati-dagdha-tanuruhasya.
Tvat-pada-padma-sarasi-ruham-agatasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam. || 3 ||

Samsara-jala-patitasya Jagan-nivasa
Sarva-indriya-artha-badisha-agra-jhashopmasya.
Protkampita-prachura-taluka-mastakasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam. || 4 ||

Samsara-kupam-ati-ghoram-agadha-mulam
Samprapya Duhkha-shata-sarpa-samaakulasya.
Dinasya Deva Kripaya Padam-agatasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam. || 5 ||

Samsara-bhikara-karindra-kara-abhighata-
Nishpidyamana-vapushah Sakala-arti-nasha.
Prana-prayana-bhava-bhiti-samaakulasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam. || 6 ||

Samsara-sarpa-visha-digdha-mahogra-tivra-
Damshtra-agra-koti-paridashta-vinashta-murteh.
Nagari-vahana Sudhabdhi-nivasa Shaure
Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam. || 7 ||

Samsara-vriksham-agha-bijam-ananta-karma-
Shakhayutam Karana-patram-ananga-pushpam.
Aruhya Duhkha-phalitam Patato Dayalo
Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam. || 8 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Lakshmi-Nrisimha-charanabja-madhu-vratena
Stotram Kritam Shubhakaram Bhuvi Shankarena.
Ye Tat-pathanti Manuja Hari-bhakti-yuktah
Te Yanti Tat-pada-sarojam-akhanda-rupam.

|| Iti Shri Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: लक्ष्मी नरसिंह करावलम्ब स्तोत्र आदि शंकराचार्य द्वारा रचित अत्यन्त प्रसिद्ध स्तोत्र है। प्रत्येक श्लोक में 'लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्' — 'हे लक्ष्मीनरसिंह, मुझे अपने हाथ का सहारा दीजिए' — यह करुण प्रार्थना है। संसार को दावानल, जाल, कूप, सर्प, भयानक हाथी और वृक्ष की उपमाओं से दर्शाया गया है। जो हरिभक्तियुक्त मनुष्य इसका पाठ करते हैं वे अखण्ड पद को प्राप्त होते हैं।
benefits: Invokes divine hand of support (karavalambam) from lord narasimha
Traditionally invoked for protection from all worldly fears compared to fire, snake, and elephant
Invokes the blessing of both lakshmi's wealth and narasimha's protection together
Composed by Adi Shankaracharya - extremely powerful for liberation
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sorrows and leads to the supreme abode
description: Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram — लक्ष्मी नरसिंह स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Narasimha. This sacred composition is sourced from Adi Shankaracharya and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to protect from all worldly fears compared to fire, snake, and elephant as well as bestow both Lakshmi's wealth and Narasimha's protection together. Additionally, it is known to grant divine hand of support (Karavalambam) from Lord Narasimha. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram by Adi Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with meaning. Prayer for divine protection and liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram and how is it related to the Karavalamba Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram (लक्ष्मी नरसिंह स्तोत्रम्) is an abridged eight-verse version of Adi Shankaracharya's celebrated sixteen-verse Lakshmi-Nrisimha Karavalamba Stotram, prefaced by an additional Lakshmi-kantam Nrisimham dhyana shloka. It preserves the opening eight verses of the original Karavalamba (Shrimat-payonidhi-niketana through the samsara-vriksha verse) along with the closing phalashruti, providing a shorter ten-minute parayana option for daily nityapaath. The text retains the signature refrain 'Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam' (O Lakshmi-Narasimha, give me the support of your hand) in every verse. The colophon and benefits are identical to the longer version. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya; classical Vaishnava nityapaath compendia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the opening Lakshmi-kantam Nrisimham dhyana shloka?
seoFaqs.answer: Lakshmi-kantam Nrisimham cha sarvaloka-eka-nayakam, vamange Lakshmi-sahitam dhyayen-nityam Harim param is the dhyana shloka prefixed to the eight-verse Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram. It instructs the devotee to meditate daily on Hari Narasimha — beloved of Lakshmi (Lakshmi-kantam), the man-lion (Nrisimham), the one and only lord of all worlds (sarvaloka-eka-nayakam), accompanied by Lakshmi seated on his left lap (vamange Lakshmi-sahitam). This iconographic description corresponds to the classical Lakshmi-Narasimha murti found in shrines like the Ahobilam Lakshmi-Narasimha temple in Andhra Pradesh and the Yadagirigutta Narasimha temple, where Narasimha is shown in his peaceful (shanta) form with Lakshmi on his lap, distinguished from the fierce Ugra Narasimha forms. (Sources: classical Vaishnava dhyana paddhati; Lakshmi-Narasimha iconography.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of Lakshmi being seated with Narasimha?
seoFaqs.answer: The presence of Goddess Lakshmi seated on Narasimha's lap transforms the iconography from Ugra Narasimha (the fierce slayer of Hiranyakashipu) into Lakshmi-Narasimha or Shanta Narasimha (the peaceful, householder form). According to the Bhagavata Purana 7.10 and Narasimha Purana, after Narasimha killed Hiranyakashipu his fury could not be pacified by any of the gods or sages — only Lakshmi (in some accounts Prahlada) approaching him cooled his rage. From that moment onward, the Lakshmi-Narasimha form became the auspicious worship-form for grihasthas (householders), combining Lakshmi's grace (wealth, auspiciousness, peace, marital harmony) with Narasimha's grace (fearlessness, protection from enemies, removal of obstacles). (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.10; Narasimha Purana; classical Vaishnava theology.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram is suitable for daily morning recitation, taking about ten minutes — making it a more compact alternative to the full sixteen-verse Karavalamba for those seeking a daily nityapaath. It is especially auspicious on Saturdays (Shanivar — when Narasimha is invoked to pacify Saturn), Thursdays (Guruvar — sacred to Vishnu), and Fridays (Shukravar — sacred to Lakshmi). Major occasions include Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi at sunset), Diwali Lakshmi Puja (Kartika Amavasya — combining Lakshmi worship with Narasimha protection), Akshaya Tritiya, every Ekadashi tithi, and Swati nakshatra (Narasimha's birth nakshatra). The dhyana shloka should be recited first, followed by the eight verses and closing phalashruti, with a ghee lamp lit before the murti. (Sources: classical Vaishnava nityapaath; Narasimha sadhana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram bestow?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti — identical to the longer Karavalamba — promises that Hari-bhakti-yukta devotees who recite this stotra attain the akhanda (unbroken, eternal) form of Lakshmi-Narasimha's lotus feet, that is, moksha through residence in Vaikuntha. Traditional commentaries assign additional benefits derived from the combined grace of Lakshmi and Narasimha: prosperity and wealth (Lakshmi-grace) along with fearlessness and protection (Narasimha-grace), harmony in family and marital life, removal of all worldly fears (samsara metaphorised as fire, net, well, elephant, serpent and tree), protection from premature death and Yama's messengers, healing of grave illnesses, victory over enemies and asuric forces, and the company of the great Vaishnava bhaktas (smarana-fruit). (Sources: phalashruti commentary; Adi Shankara karavalamba tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Lakshmi Narasimha and Ugra Narasimha worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Lakshmi Narasimha (also Shanta or Yoga Narasimha) is the peaceful, householder form of Narasimha with Lakshmi seated on his lap or by his side, worshipped primarily for prosperity, marital harmony, family welfare, fearlessness and gentle protection. This is the recommended grihastha (householder) worship-form. Ugra Narasimha is the fierce, lion-faced, blood-stained, intestine-garlanded form depicted at the moment of Hiranyakashipu's slaying, worshipped primarily for the destruction of enemies, removal of grave dangers, victory in legal and worldly conflicts, and the breaking of black-magic. Ugra worship is traditionally undertaken with stricter ritual purity and often only by initiated sadhakas. The Lakshmi Narasimha Stotram, by virtue of including Lakshmi from the very dhyana, places this text firmly in the Shanta-Narasimha grihastha tradition. (Sources: Pancharatra Agama; Vaikhanasa Agama; Narasimha Purana.)
